title: AI job retry/cancel handlers silently swallow errors

findings.description: The `handleReplayAiJob` and `handleCancelAiJob` functions have no `catch` block. If `backend.replayAiJob()` or `backend.cancelAiJob()` throws, the error propagates unhandled to the `finally` block (which only clears the action state), and the user sees no feedback about the failure. Compare with `handleRetryRuntimeJob` and `handleCancelRuntimeJob` directly below, which do have `catch` blocks that call `setPageError(message)`. This is an inconsistent error-handling pattern where AI job mutations fail silently while runtime job mutations surface errors.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a `catch` block to both handlers matching the pattern used by `handleRetryRuntimeJob`: catch the error, call `describeError()`, and set `setPageError(message)` so the user sees feedback.
